title: Round to nearest 10 or 100 on the number line
description: Practice rounding to the nearest ten and rounding to the nearest hundred on the number line.
topic_path: Get ready for addition, subtraction, and estimation
Round to nearest 10 or 100 on the number line

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is $\redD{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest ten?**   

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]

**What is $\redD{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest hundred?**   

[[☃ numeric-input 2]]

[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
First, let's look at the $\blueD{\text{tens}}$.

$\redD{\text{A}}$ is between $\blueD{220}$ and $\blueD{230}$ but closer to $\blueD{230}$.  So, $\redD{\text{A}}$ rounds up to  $\blueD{230}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Next, let's look at the $\greenD{\text{hundreds}}$.

$\redD{\text{A}}$ is between $\greenD{200}$ and $\greenD{300}$ but closer to $\greenD{200}$.  So, $\redD{\text{A}}$ rounds down to  $\greenD{200}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\redD{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest ten is $\blueD{230}$.

$\redD{\text{A}}$ rounded to the nearest hundred is $\greenD{200}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 200 to 300 with tick marks at every 5 units. The tick marks at 200, 210, 220, 230, 240, 250, 260, 270, 280, 290, and 300 are labeled. A red circle labeled A is between 220 tick mark and 230 tick mark.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Drag the point to $56$ on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]


**What is $56$ rounded to the *nearest ten*?**   
[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
First, let's graph $\greenD{56}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, let's look at the $\blue{\text{tens}}$.   

$\greenD{56}$ is between $\blue{50}$ and $\blue{60}$ but closer to $\blue{60}$.  So, $\greenD{56}$ rounds up to $\blue{60}$.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{56}$ rounded to the nearest ten is $\blue{60}$.

[[☃ input-number 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Drag the point to ${945}$ on the number line.**

[[☃ number-line 1]]


**What is ${945}$ rounded to the *nearest ten*?**   
[[☃ input-number 1]]
hints:
First, let's graph $\greenD{945}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

Now, let's look at the $\blue{\text{tens}}$.   

$\greenD{945}$ is in the middle of $\blue{940}$ and $\blue{950}$ .  When a number is the same distance from both tens, we round up.

So, $\greenD{945}$ rounds up to $\blue{950}$.
[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenD{945}$ rounded to the nearest ten is $\blue{950}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which point shows $238$ on the number line?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

![A number line from 200 to 400 with tick marks every 10 units and with 3 points. Point A is slightly to the right of the 220 tick mark. Point B is slightly to the left of the 240 tick mark. Point C is slightly to the left of the 340 tick mark.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/73e0ef5ecdf90260452cadf36edaef5622ce77a2)

**What is $238$ rounded to the *nearest hundred*?**   

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
Point $\blueD{\text{B}}$ shows $\blueD{238}$ on the number line.

![The same number line as above, with only point B labeled.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7c3c52eb9266533bf846c2b03fe6319dcba2b41a)

Now, let's look at the $\redD{\text{hundreds}}$.

$\blueD{238}$ is between $\redD{200}$ and $\redD{300}$ but closer to $\redD{200}$.  So, $\blueD{238}$ rounds down to $\redD{200}$.

![The same number line as above, with the ticks at 200 and 300 emphasized.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/51cdeb5953e4fd7fb44b27504ce6eeb2ae9feaaf)

Point $\blueD{\text{B}}$ shows $\blueD{238}$ on the number line.

$\blueD{238}$ rounded to the nearest hundred is $\redD{200}$.

choices.content: Point $\greenD{\text{A}}$
Point $\blueD{\text{B}}$
Point $\purpleD{\text{C}}$

[[☃ image 1]]

Point $\purpleD{\text{C}}$ shows $\purpleD{352}$ on the number line.

$\purpleD{352}$ rounded to the nearest hundred is $\redD{400}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 200 to 400 with tick marks at every 10 units. The tick marks at 200, 250, 300, 350, and 400 are labeled. Point A appears between 250 and 300 tick marks. Point B appears between 300 and 350 tick marks. Point C appears between 350 and 400 tick marks.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which number line highlights all whole numbers that round to $470$ if we are rounding to the nearest ten?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\blueD{465}$ is the smallest whole number that rounds to $470$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD{474}$ is the greatest whole number that rounds to $470$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, any whole number from $\blueD{465}$ to $\blueD{474}$ rounds to $470$.
The answer:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A vertical number line labeled 460 to 480 with tick marks at every 1 unit. The tick marks at 460, 465, 470, 474, and 480 are labeled. The tick marks for 465 and 474 are blue. A blue line runs along the number line from the tick mark at 465 to the tick mark at 474.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc1fc8559ef1813b74d7d7b43ccc42309493a1c0)

![A vertical number line labeled 460 to 480 with tick marks at every 1 unit. The tick marks at 460, 465, 470, 475, and 480 are labeled. The tick marks for 465 and 475 are blue. A blue line runs along the number line from the tick mark at 465 to the tick mark at 475.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8411b7e77e0f339b8194fd6b20f66e04b25042ff)

![A vertical number line labeled 460 to 480 with tick marks at every 1 unit. The tick marks at 460, 470, and 480 are labeled. The tick marks for 460 and 470 are blue. A blue line runs along the number line from the tick mark at 460 to the tick mark at 470.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b0bc192892b6507b18f3c4263f90967a38646f30)
